shortDescription.text: NoSQL Injection via $where operator with unsanitized user input
Insecure Direct Object Reference allowing access to other users' data

message.text: The 'threshold' query parameter is interpolated directly into a MongoDB $where clause without sanitization. An attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript that MongoDB's SpiderMonkey engine will execute server-side. For example, supplying threshold=0';while(true){}' causes a denial-of-service, and threshold=1'; return 1 == '1 bypasses the filter to return all records. The commented-out fix (parseInt + range check) is not active.

The userId for allocation lookup is taken from req.params.userId (the URL path) instead of req.session.userId. Any authenticated user can enumerate and access other users' allocation data by changing the userId in the URL path (e.g., GET /allocations/2). The commented-out fix that reads userId from the session is not active.
